steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Line $s$ is a transversal cutting two parallel lines $q$ and $r$. The angle of 61° is marked at the intersection with line $q$ (above the line), and angle $y$ is marked at the intersection with line $r$ (in the interior region between the parallel lines, on the left side of the transversal). These are co-interior angles (also called same-side interior angles).

steps.title: Apply the co-interior angle property
steps.content:
When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, co-interior angles are supplementary—they sum to 180°. Therefore:

$$61° + y° = 180°$$

Solving for $y$:

$$y° = 180° - 61° = 119°$$

steps.title: Substitute into the equation and solve for $x$
steps.content:
We're given that $y = 2x + 9$. Since $y = 119$:

$$119 = 2x + 9$$

$$110 = 2x$$

$$x = 55$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
